manifest.author: LiveAQuietLife

manifest.name: Hold Course

name: Hold Course

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
This release covers everything since 1.1.2. Versions 1.2.0, 1.2.1, and 1.3.0
were built but never published, so their changes are included here.

## Bulk entry

**Lectures.** A "Bulk add" button on the Lectures tab opens a paste box for
entering many lectures at once. Lectures can be dated automatically from a start
date and the class meeting days, with dates walking the calendar from there. A
blank line skips one meeting, for breaks. A date at the end of a line marks an
out-of-band session — a guest or makeup lecture — without consuming a meeting
slot. ISO (`2026-08-24`), `Aug 24` / `24 Aug`, and numeric (`8/24`) date formats
are accepted. A live preview shows every parsed lecture and its date before
anything is added.

Short titles are flagged in the preview for a second look, since a pasted line
break can split one lecture into two. The preview also notes that a bulk add
cannot be undone in a single step.

**Assignments.** A "Bulk add" button in the lecture view opens a paste box for
entering many assignments at once. Each line becomes one assignment attached to
that lecture and inherits the lecture's date as its due date. One type — Reading,
Writing, Project, Discussion, or Other — is chosen for the whole batch. A live
preview shows every assignment before anything is added.

## Class contacts and meeting link

A class can now record office hours for the professor, along with a name, email,
and office hours for a teaching assistant. The class header shows the professor
and the TA side by side as labeled blocks of equal weight, with a divider between
them. Everything is optional and nothing is drawn until it has content: a person
appears only once a field is filled in, the TA block is absent entirely until it
has something to show, and the divider appears only when there are two blocks to
divide.

A class can also hold a recurring meeting link — Zoom or similar — kept separate
from the existing course page URL and grouped with the meeting days it belongs
to. It appears in the class header when set.

## Fixed

**Class cards no longer stretch to fit long assignment titles.** A card whose
next assignment had a long title would widen its own column, leaving the two
Overview cards unequal, and the title's truncation never took effect. Cards now
hold an even half-width and long titles truncate as intended.

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
## [1.1.2] - 2026-07-06

### Fixed
- Assignment status pill moved from the title column to the right-aligned column, matching where status already lives on lecture and exam rows. It previously sat under the assignment title, inconsistent with the other two list types and easy to miss.

## [1.1.1] - 2026-07-06

### Fixed
- The 1.1.0 GitHub release was missing its binary files (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`). No code changes from 1.1.0.

## [1.1.0] - 2026-07-06

### Added
- Overdue column on the dashboard's Today strip. Renders only when at least one assignment is past due, in the first position, capped at five items with a "+N more overdue" indicator.
- In-list status cycling. Status chips on lecture rows, class-tab assignment rows, and the global Assignments list are now clickable and cycle through Not started ? In progress ? Done without opening the detail screen.
- Two-state status toggle on exam rows, matching the exam detail screen's Mark done / Done model.
- Rename semester, available from the semester dropdown.
- Delete semester, available from the semester dropdown. The confirmation names exactly what will be removed (classes, lectures, assignments, exams, and library resources). Deleting the active semester falls back to the next remaining semester.
- Grade chips on completed rows. When a done assignment or exam has a grade recorded, it appears as a quiet muted pill in the class-tab list, the global Assignments list, and exam rows.
- Overnight refresh for the Today sidebar. It re-renders when the date rolls over so Today and Tomorrow stay accurate without a manual reload.

### Changed
- Dark theme support. Accent colors used as text (class codes, breadcrumbs, meta links, tabs, dropdown labels) and amber urgency colors now use brightened variants when a dark theme is active. Pastel type and status pills are unchanged.

### Fixed
- Overdue assignments no longer disappear from the dashboard. Items past their due date were previously excluded from both "Due today" and "Coming up"; they now surface in the dedicated Overdue column.

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
[1.1.1] - [2026-07-06]
Fixed
- Release assets were missing from the 1.1.0 GitHub release binary. No code changes.

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
## [1.1.0] - 2026-07-06

### Added
- Overdue column on the dashboard's Today strip. Renders only when at least one assignment is past due, in the first position, capped at five items with a "+N more overdue" indicator.
- In-list status cycling. Status chips on lecture rows, class-tab assignment rows, and the global Assignments list are now clickable and cycle through Not started > In progress > Done without opening the detail screen.
- Two-state status toggle on exam rows, matching the exam detail screen's Mark done / Done model.
- Rename semester, available from the semester dropdown.
- Delete semester, available from the semester dropdown. The confirmation names exactly what will be removed (classes, lectures, assignments, exams, and library resources). Deleting the active semester falls back to the next remaining semester.
- Grade chips on completed rows. When a done assignment or exam has a grade recorded, it appears as a quiet muted pill in the class-tab list, the global Assignments list, and exam rows.
- Overnight refresh for the Today sidebar. It re-renders when the date rolls over so Today and Tomorrow stay accurate without a manual reload.

### Changed
- Better dark theme support. Accent colors used as text (class codes, breadcrumbs, meta links, tabs, dropdown labels) and amber urgency colors now use brightened variants when a dark theme is active. Pastel type and status pills are unchanged.

### Fixed
- Overdue assignments no longer disappear from the dashboard. Items past their due date were previously excluded from both "Due today" and "Coming up"; they now surface in the dedicated Overdue column.

## [1.0.1] - 2026-06-28

### Added
- Optional course page URL on classes. Appears as an external-link icon on the dashboard card and a "Course page" link in the class header.
- "Add a lecture" command in the command palette. Adds a lecture to the class currently in view.

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Course page URL**: Optional URL field on each class. Renders as a quiet external-link icon on dashboard cards and as a "Course page" link in the Class view header. No migration required; existing classes without the field are unaffected.
- **"Add a lecture" command**: New command palette entry. Opens the Add Lecture modal against the current class. Requires an active semester and an open class screen; shows a notice otherwise.
- **"Add an assignment" command**:  New command palette entry. Opens the Add Assignment modal against the current class. Same guards as the lecture command.

### Bug Fixes
- **Due-today items disappearing in the evening**: Items due today would vanish from the Today sidebar and Overview strip after 8 PM for users in negative UTC offset time zones (e.g. US Eastern).

releases.author: LiveAQuietLife
releases.description:
Hold Course 1.0.0
Academic course and assignment tracker for Obsidian.
Track classes, lectures, assignments, exams, and library resources across semesters. Includes a Today sidebar, global assignments view, calendar, and command palette shortcuts — all in a single desktop plugin.
